links.targetTitle: Live collaboration uses per-user op feeds plus GSOC pings to trigger debounced pulls.
Live collab uses per-user op feeds plus ACT-gated collab manifest
Chat 1-to-1 PSS messaging with feed-indexed history shipped

global.title: Live collaboration via ACT + GSOC is the next major track
global.content:
## The next track

Live collaboration via ACT + GSOC is the remaining major track on the chat roadmap as of 2026-04-18. Detailed plan in `live-collaboration-design.md`.

## What "live" means

Both users have the document open. When one commits an op, the other sees it appear in the **toolbar's History view within a second or two**, attributed to the correct signer.

## Explicit non-goals

- **No cursor overlays.**
- **No caret labels.**
- **No per-editor presence.**

Powerhouse editors are too varied -- tables, visual canvases, dashboards, button grids -- for any of that to be meaningful across the surface.

## Supporting foundation

Signatures are already preserved end-to-end (shipped 2026-04-17), so the toolbar history shows the real author of every revision as soon as the pull mechanism lands. That's what makes attribution in live collab trivially correct.
global.description: Live collab scope: both users see committed ops in toolbar History within 1-2s with correct signer. No cursor overlays or per-editor presence.
